sourceMovie.title: I, Robot
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto I, Robot (2004) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Chappie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I, Robot clicked for you, Chappie (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after I, Robot?
faq.answer: Try Chappie, The Matrix Resurrections, Upgrade—each shares DNA with I, Robot while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I, Robot?
faq.answer: I, Robot (2004) is best described as Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I, Robot worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
